relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul refuses credential theater as the basis of trust.
The community itself is the letter written in hearts.
Messiah’s letter is written by Spirit on human hearts, not ink or stone.
Confidence rests through Messiah toward the Divine.
Sufficiency does not come from the self.
The Divine makes servants of new covenant: Spirit making alive beyond letter alone.
The old glory around Moses was real, even as it was passing.
The service of Spirit carries surpassing glory.
Righteousness/justice ministry abounds beyond condemnation ministry.
Surpassing glory relativizes former glory without denying it.
What remains in Spirit carries greater glory.
The veil image names hidden completion.
In Messiah, the veil is made inactive.
The heart-veil image remains part of Paul’s rhetoric.
Turning to the Lord removes the veil.
Where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
With unveiled face, the community beholds and reflects glory, being transformed from glory into glory.

contemplative_note: 2 Corinthians 3 is heart-tablet transformation. Spirit writes living letters, freedom unveils the face, and glory becomes a mirror that changes the beholder.
